title: carpinus pubescens var. seemeniana
intro: Carpinus pubescens var. seemeniana, also known as Korean hornbeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, specifically in China and Korea. Traditionally, Korean hornbeam has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties. In herbal preparations, Korean hornbeam is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in combination with other herbs to treat specific health conditions, such as respiratory issues and skin problems.
